summary: Question 1.1 from arXiv:1708.07369 asks whether every finite family of graphs containing at least one forest is k-nice for all sufficiently large k. The source paper proves this for families containing a forest with at most 2 edges and for each of the three two-graph families of connected 3-edge graphs. A targeted search across arXiv and the published literature found no follow-up paper resolving the full conjecture, and the arXiv full-text search for 'Ramsey-nice k-nice forest' returned zero results beyond the original paper.
search_queries: Ramsey-nice families graphs k-nice forest conjecture Aharoni Alon 1708.07369
Ramsey-nice graph family forest k-nice eventually arXiv
notes: No follow-up resolving the conjecture was found. The arXiv full-text search returned zero results for the distinctive terminology 'Ramsey-nice k-nice forest'. The paper itself was published in European Journal of Combinatorics 72 (2018) 29–44. Question 1.1 remains open as of 2026-05-15.
